    "stop-grouting-work",
    "remove-misapplied-grout"

  "findings": [
    "Epoxy vs. cementitious grout misapplied under the equipment baseplate",
    "Grouting procedure is out of sequence and violates engineering specifications"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Issue an immediate stop-work order on the grouting activity",
    "Chop out and completely remove the wet or partially cured misapplied grout",
    "Clean and prepare the concrete foundation and baseplate surfaces again",
    "Verify the correct grout material (epoxy vs. cementitious) against API RP 686 and engineering drawings before re-pouring"
  ],
  "rationale": "Applying the incorrect type of grout (epoxy instead of cementitious, or vice versa) compromises the structural integrity, vibration damping, and load transfer of the machinery baseplate. Because the work is actively in progress, it must be halted immediately to remove the incorrect material before it fully cures, preventing severe damage to the foundation and avoiding costly, difficult rework later.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 686 Chapter 5 (Mounting Plate Grouting)"
  ]
}
